page: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 server family. It is powered by an x86_64 architecture Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 614 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations place the D16ds_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1899.98, while the multi-core score reaches 15531.7. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 833.76 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 58.27 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the server handles 15.82 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a static web serving throughput of 21.35 GB/sec. For lightweight language model tasks, it demonstrates strong text generation performance of 265.09 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16ds_v5 offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated compute cores, moderate memory capacity, and fast local storage without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The inclusion of a 614 GB local disk provides a cost-effective solution for workloads requiring temporary storage or caching. This virtual machine is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, medium-sized databases, web serving, software compilation, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance includes 614 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks a dedicated GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate average tier capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9821.0 and a static web serving throughput of 21.35 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in lightweight LLM text generation, reaching 265.09 tokens per second on a 135M model. This server provides a cost-effective balance of compute, memory, and local storage, making it highly suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, designed for balanced database and web workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, fast local storage, and reliable performance for database and web applications.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and a 614 GB local storage drive.
Delivers 15.82 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks.
Achieves 21.35 GB/sec throughput for static web serving workloads.
Suited for general-purpose computing, web serving, and database operations.
